content_sha256_note: sha256 over canonical JSON of {task_id, class, items} only (sorted keys, no whitespace) -- the hash that must not change once this checklist is frozen; recompute and compare before trusting any copy of this file.

items.description: A `report` command exists on the CLI group, taking one required transactions-CSV path argument, and supports a `--by-category` flag.
items.how_to_detect: Inspect src/ledgerkit/cli.py for a new click command named report registered on the cli group with a --by-category option; invoke it via CliRunner with a sample CSV and confirm it runs.

items.description: With --by-category, output is one line per category with a non-empty total, formatted `<category>: <amount>` using format_cents (never a raw float or unformatted int).
items.how_to_detect: Run report --by-category against a CSV touching several categorized accounts; confirm each printed line matches the category/amount shape and the amount string matches format_cents' fixed two-decimal output for that total.

items.description: Lines are sorted by descending total (largest first); a tie in total is broken by ascending category name.
items.how_to_detect: Construct a CSV producing at least two categories with different totals and, separately, a case with two categories tied at the same total; confirm output order matches descending-total-then-ascending-name.

items.description: When there are no categorized entries (empty CSV, or a CSV that only touches uncategorized accounts), the command prints a clear no-data message and exits 0 (not an error).
items.how_to_detect: Run report --by-category against a CSV with no categorized-account entries; confirm exit code is 0 and the output communicates there is nothing to report rather than printing an empty table silently or raising.

items.description: A missing/unreadable CSV file, or a CSV row referencing an unknown account id, makes the command exit non-zero rather than crash with an unhandled traceback.
items.how_to_detect: Run report --by-category against a nonexistent path and against a CSV referencing an account id absent from the demo chart; confirm both exit non-zero and neither produces a raw unhandled-exception traceback in the CLI output.

items.description: The existing `balance` command's behavior and output are unchanged.
items.how_to_detect: Diff cli.py's balance_cmd and re-run the existing balance-command tests; confirm no change to its output shape or exit codes.

items.description: New tests cover: normal multi-category descending output, a tie broken alphabetically, the empty/no-categorized-entries case, and at least one failure case (missing file or unknown account) -- and the full test suite and lint gate pass.
items.how_to_detect: Read tests/test_cli.py (or wherever the new tests land) for coverage of all four cases; run `pytest tests/` and `ruff check .` from the fixture root and confirm both succeed.
